title: Primary, secondary and tertiary education enrollment and completion rates
subtitle: This is given as the gross rate, which includes children of any age entering the level of education; this can result in percentages greater than 100 because children may enter education late or repeat a year.
caption:
Primary, secondary and tertiary education enrollment and completion rates, World

This is given as the gross rate, which includes children of any age entering the level of education; this can result in percentages greater than 100 because children may enter education late or repeat a year.

The line chart tracks global education metrics from 1970 to 2024, measuring the gross enrollment and completion rates for primary, secondary, and tertiary levels. The vertical axis ranges from 0% to over 100%, while the horizontal axis spans five decades. Primary enrollment, represented by the top blue line, remains the highest metric throughout the period, starting near 90% in 1970, rising to a peak above 100% around 2008, and fluctuating slightly above the 100% mark through 2024. Primary completion, shown in green from 2000 onward, begins just under 80% and climbs steadily to approximately 90% by 2024. Secondary enrollment, depicted in orange, starts near 40% in 1970, dips slightly in the early 1980s, and then rises consistently to reach roughly 80% by the end of the timeline. Lower secondary completion, the purple line appearing in 2000, tracks closely with secondary enrollment, starting near 60% and rising to just under 80% by 2024. Tertiary enrollment, the brown line at the bottom, begins near 10% in 1970 and shows a slow increase until 2000, after which the slope steepens significantly, reaching just above 40% in 2024. The data shows that while primary participation has remained near universal for decades, tertiary education has seen the most rapid relative growth in recent years, though it remains the lowest rate among the tracked categories.
